title: Chhath Puja
intro: Chhath Puja is one of the most sacred and ancient Hindu festivals dedicated to Surya Dev (Sun God) and Chhathi Maiya.
description:
Chhath Puja is one of the most sacred and ancient Hindu festivals dedicated to Surya Dev (Sun God) and Chhathi Maiya. It is celebrated mainly in Bihar, Jharkhand, Uttar Pradesh, and parts of Nepal, with great devotion and spiritual discipline. The festival is also celebrated by communities across India and abroad. Chhath Puja is observed on the Shukla Paksha Shashthi Tithi of the Hindu month of Kartik, usually during October or November.

The festival is a unique form of Sun worship, where devotees express gratitude to Surya Dev, the source of life, energy, and health. Devotees pray for the well-being of their families, prosperity, good health, and the happiness of their children. Chhath Puja is known for its strict rituals, purity, fasting, and deep faith.

According to Hindu traditions, Chhathi Maiya is worshipped as a divine mother who blesses devotees with protection, happiness, and prosperity. The festival also has connections with ancient Sun worship traditions and is mentioned in various Hindu beliefs and legends.

Chhath Puja is celebrated for four days, each having special significance:

* Nahay Khay: Devotees begin the festival by taking a holy bath and consuming a pure vegetarian meal after cleaning their homes and surroundings.
* Kharna: Devotees observe a day-long fast and break it in the evening after offering prayers. A special Prasad of jaggery kheer, fruits, and traditional food is prepared.
* Sandhya Arghya: Devotees gather at rivers, ponds, and Chhath Ghats to offer water and prayers to the setting Sun.
* Usha Arghya: On the final day, devotees offer prayers to the rising Sun and complete the sacred fast.

A special offering called Thekua, along with fruits, sugarcane, and traditional Prasad, is prepared and offered to Surya Dev and Chhathi Maiya. The festival represents faith, discipline, gratitude towards nature, and the importance of family bonds.

Festival Month

Kartik (October – November)
(Chaiti Chhath is also celebrated during the Chaitra month in some regions.)

Significance / Importance

Chhath Puja holds immense religious, cultural, and spiritual importance. Surya Dev is worshipped as the visible form of God and the giver of life, light, energy, and health.

Devotees believe that observing the Chhath fast with devotion brings blessings of happiness, prosperity, protection, and family well-being. The strict rituals of fasting and cleanliness represent self-discipline, purity of mind, and devotion.

Unlike many festivals, Chhath Puja does not involve elaborate idols. Devotees worship nature, water, and the Sun directly, making it a festival that reflects environmental respect and gratitude towards natural elements.

The festival also promotes equality, as people from all communities gather together at Chhath Ghats to offer prayers to the Sun.

Puja Vidhi / Rituals

Day 1: Nahay Khay

* Devotees clean their homes and surroundings.
* After bathing, they prepare pure vegetarian food.
* The devotee begins the Chhath Vrat with devotion and purity.

Day 2: Kharna

* Devotees observe a complete fast during the day.
* In the evening, after worship, Prasad is prepared and offered.
* The fast is broken with traditional food.

Day 3: Sandhya Arghya

* Devotees visit Chhath Ghats near rivers, ponds, or water bodies.
* They offer fruits, lamps, and Prasad to the setting Sun.
* Traditional Chhath songs are sung during the rituals.

Day 4: Usha Arghya

* Devotees offer prayers to the rising Sun.
* The final offerings are made, and the fast is completed.
* Blessings are sought for family happiness and prosperity.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Chhath Puja Vrat Katha
* Chhathi Maiya Katha
* Surya Dev Katha
* Surya Mantra
* Aditya Hridaya Stotra
* Surya Aarti
* Gayatri Mantra

faq.a1: Chhath Puja is celebrated on the Shashthi Tithi of Shukla Paksha in the Hindu month of Kartik, usually in October or November.
faq.q2: Who is worshipped during Chhath Puja?
faq.a2: Devotees worship Surya Dev (Sun God) and Chhathi Maiya during this festival.
faq.q3: Where is Chhath Puja mainly celebrated?
faq.a3: Chhath Puja is mainly celebrated in Bihar, Jharkhand, Uttar Pradesh, and parts of Nepal.
faq.q4: How many days is Chhath Puja celebrated?
faq.a4: Chhath Puja is a four-day festival with Nahay Khay, Kharna, Sandhya Arghya, and Usha Arghya as the main rituals.
faq.q5: Why is the Sun worshipped during Chhath Puja?
faq.a5: The Sun is worshipped as the source of life, energy, health, and prosperity.
faq.a6: Chhathi Maiya is a revered divine mother worshipped during Chhath Puja for blessings, protection, and family well-being.
faq.q7: What food is prepared during Chhath Puja?
faq.a7: Thekua, fruits, sugarcane, jaggery-based sweets, and traditional Prasad are prepared during Chhath Puja.
faq.q8: What is the significance of offering Arghya to the Sun?
faq.a8: Offering Arghya to the rising and setting Sun represents gratitude, devotion, and respect towards the source of life.
faq.q9: Is Chhath Puja celebrated outside India?
faq.a9: Yes, Chhath Puja is celebrated by Indian communities in many countries around the world.
faq.q10: What is the spiritual message of Chhath Puja?
faq.a10: Chhath Puja teaches faith, purity, self-discipline, gratitude towards nature, and devotion towards the divine.
